content: @Miguel Ojeda any reason the rustc opt level is set to 2? Cargo defaults to 3 for release builds. I was told the kernel cannot be built with gcc opt level 3 because some C stuff will break. But for Rust code opt level 3 should be OK?
We should keep it consistent with clang's opt level, because MIR-wise there's no difference between 2 and 3, the only difference is the passes that LLVM uses.
@Andreas Hindborg any chance you could submit your patch to add the compiler flag needed for lto?

@Andreas Hindborg The kernel under LLVM also uses -O2 (or -Os), thus we use that one too for Rust. We had the other optimization levels previously, but they were removed after discussion with Nick Desaulniers pre-merge. See commit fee989b66973 on rust, I saved some Links there to lore:

https://lore.kernel.org/lkml/CAKwvOdkjttdX83tL4pw+J5EnHM1MgEYDPp=YTpEagV4RrhdxwA@mail.gmail.com/
https://lore.kernel.org/lkml/CAKwvOd=7QTUH69+ZbT7e8einvgcosTbDkyohmPaUBv6_y8RfrQ@mail.gmail.com/
https://lore.kernel.org/lkml/CANiq72kySVvOQ7eqwe0Jzz3V0JTtrcqODHR9Ty4-sfDMdzP6XQ@mail.gmail.com/
https://lore.kernel.org/lkml/CAKwvOdn+9qORm8UpDGnPXxiK7B7P_TW5CtXv1+8qkv7UvQr3hQ@mail.gmail.com/
https://lore.kernel.org/lkml/CAKwvOdkTjxNEmCTnuH5f41WB50ef6ErBM2Kp2zJ-t9q_5U8rBA@mail.gmail.com/

If you have a use case (e.g. you see better performance in your driver), then we may have a case to bring it back.

Having said that, please note that the kernel also removed CONFIG_CC_OPTIMIZE_FOR_PERFORMANCE_O3, it is not even there for LLVM builds. See commit a6036a41bffb on mainline for that one. Nick says there:

    Code that looks to re-add an explicit Kconfig option for `-O3` should
    provide:
    1. A rigorous and reproducible performance profile of a reasonable
       userspace workload that demonstrates a hot loop in the kernel that
       would benefit from `-O3` over `-O2`.
    2. Disassembly of said loop body before and after.
    3. Provides stats on terms of increase in file size.

Wedson Almeida Filho said:

Andreas Hindborg any chance you could submit your patch to add the compiler flag needed for lto?

When do you need it? I can dump a draft here if you want.

For the kernel elf its simple, but for loadable modules it's a bit tricky because the stuff you want to inline is actually in the main kernel elf. I get around it by duplicating helpers in a C file that I link with my Rust module. The process is further complicated because the linker for some reason is not performing inlining at link time unless I do some trickery to the bitcode first.
What I really want is to extract he functions I need in my module from helpers.o and include them in the link of my module. I have no idea how to do that though.
